skill.name: agent-memory
skill.description: Provides a persistent Markdown memory folder for saving, organizing, searching, and maintaining reusable notes across AI sessions.
skill.summary: Save and search persistent AI memory notes across conversations.
skill.author: yamadashy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, weak-crypto terms, and system-reconnaissance patterns, but review found no cryptography, network access, or prompt injection. The confirmed risk is limited to documented local shell commands that search, create, write, and remove memory files. The skill is safe to publish with a low-risk warning for local filesystem changes.

low_findings.title: Local Filesystem Modification Workflow
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to create directories, write memory files, and remove obsolete memory files under a fixed memory path. This is expected behavior, but it can alter local files if category or filename choices are incorrect.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines directly show mkdir, heredoc file writing, trash, and rmdir commands. The commands are scoped to the documented memory directory, so the risk is limited.

low_findings.title: Local Shell Search Commands
low_findings.description: The skill provides ls, rg, and date command examples for searching memory files and creating dated frontmatter. These are legitimate local operations, but they depend on shell tools and local path handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are visible in Markdown code blocks and match the skill purpose. No evidence shows command injection, networking, or privileged execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands in Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: Markdown contains bash examples for listing, searching, creating, and deleting local memory files. Static Ruby backtick and weak-crypto alerts are false positives caused by Markdown formatting and example text.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file includes executable shell examples, so the external command pattern is real. The reviewed context shows local memory maintenance rather than malicious execution.

content.value_statement: AI assistants often lose useful context between sessions. This skill stores selected notes in a searchable local memory folder.
actual_capabilities: Creates Markdown memory notes with required summary frontmatter.
Organizes memories into user-defined category folders.
Searches summaries, tags, and full text with ripgrep examples.
Updates, deletes, consolidates, and reorganizes stored memories.
Prompts proactive memory saving for reusable findings and decisions.
limitations: It stores notes as local Markdown files, not in a database.
It does not encrypt memories or classify sensitive content.
Search quality depends on clear summaries, tags, and filenames.
It requires local shell tools for the documented search workflow.

use_cases.title: Remember implementation details
use_cases.description: Save architectural choices, workarounds, and project-specific patterns for later coding sessions.

use_cases.title: Preserve investigation notes
use_cases.description: Keep important findings, source summaries, and unresolved questions available across separate conversations.

use_cases.title: Maintain reusable project context
use_cases.description: Record decisions, risks, and recurring practices that help future assistants start with better context.

prompt_templates.title: Save a simple memory
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one useful note.
prompt_templates.prompt: Remember this project rule: always run the formatter before opening a pull request.

prompt_templates.title: Recall related memories
prompt_templates.scenario: Find previous context before starting work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check your memories for anything related to our deployment workflow before you suggest changes.

prompt_templates.title: Organize saved context
prompt_templates.scenario: Clean up notes after repeated work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review memories about authentication work and consolidate related notes into a clearer category.

prompt_templates.title: Preserve a detailed decision
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced memory with context and future guidance.
prompt_templates.prompt: Save a memory explaining why we chose this caching strategy, including tradeoffs, risks, and when to revisit it.

output_examples.input: Remember that production deploys require the release checklist.
output_examples.output: The assistant saves a dated memory with a concise summary and places it in a relevant workflow category.

output_examples.input: What did we decide about large file processing?
output_examples.output: The assistant searches memory summaries first, then reads matching notes and reports the relevant decision.

output_examples.input: Clean up outdated memories about the old build tool.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies obsolete notes, removes or updates them, and leaves related current memories organized.

best_practices: Write short summaries that clearly state when the memory is useful.
Use categories and tags that match the way you search later.
Review old memories when facts change or decisions are replaced.
anti_patterns: Do not save secrets, credentials, or private personal data as memories.
Do not save every conversation detail without clear future value.
Do not overwrite existing memories before checking for related notes.

faq.question: Where does this skill store memories?
faq.answer: It stores Markdown files under the agent-memory memories folder described by the skill.

faq.question: Can it search old memories?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses summary, tag, and full-text search workflows based on local files.

faq.question: Does it sync memories to a cloud service?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflow uses local files and does not include network sync.

faq.question: Can it delete memories?
faq.answer: Yes. The maintenance workflow includes deleting obsolete memories and removing empty categories.

faq.question: Are memories encrypted?
faq.answer: No. The skill describes plain Markdown files, so sensitive information should not be stored.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
